verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur Eliu hæc rursum locutus est :
Numquid æqua tibi videtur tua cogitatio,  ut diceres : Justior sum Deo ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲ֭זֹאת חָשַׁ֣בְתָּ לְמִשְׁפָּ֑ט אָ֝מַ֗רְתָּ צִדְקִ֥י מֵאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: Thinkest thou this to be right, that thou saidst, My righteousness is more than God’s?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixisti enim : Non tibi placet quod rectum est :  vel quid tibi proderit, si ego peccavero ?
verses.king_james: For thou saidst, What advantage will it be unto thee? and, What profit shall I have, if I be cleansed from my sin?

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque ego respondebo sermonibus tuis,  et amicis tuis tecum.
verses.king_james: I will answer thee, and thy companions with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Suspice cælum, et intuere :  et contemplare æthera quod altior te sit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַבֵּ֣ט שָׁמַ֣יִם וּרְאֵ֑ה וְשׁ֥וּר שְׁ֝חָקִ֗ים גָּבְה֥וּ מִמֶּֽךָּ׃
verses.king_james: Look unto the heavens, and see; and behold the clouds which are higher than thou.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si peccaveris, quid ei nocebis ?  et si multiplicatæ fuerint iniquitates tuæ, quid facies contra eum ?
verses.king_james: If thou sinnest, what doest thou against him? or if thy transgressions be multiplied, what doest thou unto him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro si juste egeris, quid donabis ei ?  aut quid de manu tua accipiet ?
verses.king_james: If thou be righteous, what givest thou him? or what receiveth he of thine hand?

verses.latin_vulgate: Homini qui similis tui est, nocebit impietas tua :  et filium hominis adjuvabit justitia tua.
verses.king_james: Thy wickedness may hurt a man as thou art; and thy righteousness may profit the son of man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter multitudinem calumniatorum clamabunt,  et ejulabunt propter vim brachii tyrannorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵ֭רֹב עֲשׁוּקִ֣ים יַזְעִ֑יקוּ יְשַׁוְּע֖וּ מִזְּר֣וֹעַ רַבִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: By reason of the multitude of oppressions they make the oppressed to cry: they cry out by reason of the arm of the mighty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non dixit : Ubi est Deus qui fecit me,  qui dedit carmina in nocte ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽלֹא־אָמַ֗ר אַ֭יֵּה אֱל֣וֹהַּ עֹשָׂ֑י נֹתֵ֖ן זְמִר֣וֹת בַּלָּֽיְלָה׃
verses.king_james: But none saith, Where is God my maker, who giveth songs in the night;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui docet nos super jumenta terræ,  et super volucres cæli erudit nos ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַ֭לְּפֵנוּ מִבַּהֲמ֣וֹת אָ֑רֶץ וּמֵע֖וֹף הַשָּׁמַ֣יִם יְחַכְּמֵֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth, and maketh us wiser than the fowls of heaven?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ibi clamabunt, et non exaudiet,  propter superbiam malorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁ֣ם יִ֭צְעֲקוּ וְלֹ֣א יַעֲנֶ֑ה מִ֝פְּנֵ֗י גְּא֣וֹן רָעִֽים׃
verses.king_james: There they cry, but none giveth answer, because of the pride of evil men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non ergo frustra audiet Deus,  et Omnipotens causas singulorum intuebitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַךְ־שָׁ֭וְא לֹא־יִשְׁמַ֥ע ׀ אֵ֑ל וְ֝שַׁדַּ֗י לֹ֣א יְשׁוּרֶֽנָּה׃
verses.king_james: Surely God will not hear vanity, neither will the Almighty regard it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Etiam cum dixeris : Non considerat :  judicare coram illo, et expecta eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֣ף כִּֽי־תֹ֭אמַר לֹ֣א תְשׁוּרֶ֑נּוּ דִּ֥ין לְ֝פָנָ֗יו וּתְח֥וֹלֵֽל לֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Although thou sayest thou shalt not see him, yet judgment is before him; therefore trust thou in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc enim non infert furorem suum,  nec ulciscitur scelus valde.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֗ה כִּי־אַ֭יִן פָּקַ֣ד אַפּ֑וֹ וְלֹֽא־יָדַ֖ע בַּפַּ֣שׁ מְאֹֽד׃
verses.king_james: But now, because it is not so, he hath visited in his anger; yet he knoweth it not in great extremity:

verses.latin_vulgate: Ergo Job frustra aperit os suum,  et absque scientia verba multiplicat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭אִיּוֹב הֶ֣בֶל יִפְצֶה־פִּ֑יהוּ בִּבְלִי־דַ֝֗עַת מִלִּ֥ין יַכְבִּֽר׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Therefore doth Job open his mouth in vain; he multiplieth words without knowledge.
